blocks.text: DTC P2185: Radiator water temperature sensor voltage high

Radiator water temperature sensors are thermistor type, mounted on the radiator, and are used for air-fuel ratio feedback control, ignition timing control, and idle control. The PGM-FI ECU supplies voltage (approximately 5 V) to the signal circuit of the radiator water temperature sensor through a pull-up register. When the radiator water temperature is low, the electrical resistance of the radiator water temperature sensor increases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a higher voltage. As the radiator water temperature increases, the electrical resistance decreases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a lower voltage. When the radiator water temperature is low, if the radiator water temperature sensor output voltage remains above the specified value for more than the specified time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Ignition switch/ ENGINE START STOP switch
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When the radiator water temperature sensor output voltage [radiator water temperature sensor] remains above 4.92V for more than 2 seconds.

items.text: Disconnection of code (TW2 SENSOR line) between PGM-FI ECU and radiator water temperature sensor
Disconnection of code (SG line) between PGM-FI ECU and radiator water temperature sensor
Failure of radiator water temperature sensor

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
